About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data shows two distinctly different momentum profiles at the top of the rankings. FSLY (rank #1, score 94.5) demonstrates textbook consistent momentum with perfectly balanced ratio passes (5/5), strong performance across all timeframes from 1-year (534.69%) down to 1-day (3.64%), and notably resilient behavior on down SPY days (bad_spy_score -0.1877). In contrast, PL ranks second with explosive but erratic momentum—achieving an extreme 1053% one-year return while showing only 3 of 5 ratio consistency passes and a positive bad_spy_score (0.4204), indicating it tends to rise when markets fall, which may signal correlation breakdown or sector rotation rather than sustained momentum. FSLY's all-SMA gates passing combined with near-perfect ratio consistency (99.88 ratio score vs 91.74 returns score) indicates a smoother uptrend, while PL's massive gap between its 1-year return (1053%) and 3-month return (67.35%) with a ratio-1y_3m of 15.63 suggests the majority of gains occurred earlier in the year. The volatility ratio for FSLY is normal (0.969) with no pullback flag, while PL's profile suggests
